• Water was used to wash off {{BLANK}}

Ancient Greece:
• People cleaned themselves with sand and other substances
• Used a strigil – scraper made of {{BLANK}}
• Washed clothes in streams

• Used soap to colour their {{BLANK}}
Ancient Rome:
• Animal fat, ashes and clay mixed through action of rain, used for washing clothes
• From about 312 BC, water carried to Roman {{BLANK}} by aqueducts
Europe in Middle Ages:
• Decline in bathing contributed to occurrence of {{BLANK}}
• {{BLANK}} began to be added to soap
Europe from 17th century:
• 1600s: cleanliness and bathing started becoming usual
• 1791: Leblanc invented a way of making soda ash from {{BLANK}}
• Early 1800s: Chevreul turned soapmaking into a {{BLANK}}
• From 1800s, there was no longer a {{BLANK}} on soap
